mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits easier financial conditions to equities through lower discount rates and improved risk appetite, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when policy is in an easing phase.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d forward S&P 500 returns are not positive in at least 55% of months with a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or if the signal's walk-forward Rank IC stays below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is above a restrictive threshold, equity duration is penalized by higher real discount rates and tighter liquidity, so forward S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days turn negative.
failure_condition: Reject if, after FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, the next-5-day S&P 500 return is positive in more than 50% of triggered observations, or if the conditional average return is not below the unconditional sample mean by at least 0.25 standard deviations.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in PAYEMS and a stable or falling UNRATE indicates broad labor-market demand without recessionary slack, supporting earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising PAYEMS and non-rising UNRATE does not outperform either proxy alone in out-of-sample Rank IC, or if triggered 20d returns are not positive in at least 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in RSAFS signals weakening retail demand and softer revenue growth expectations, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days once the decline exceeds a short-run threshold.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS falls more than 1.0% over 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if, after RSAFS declines more than 1.0% over 2 months, the median 20d S&P 500 return is not negative, or if the hit rate for negative returns is below 55% across triggered episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO together with higher HOUST indicates improving real activity and supply-side capacity utilization, which should support cyclical earnings and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined INDPRO-HOUST signal does not improve 60d forward return prediction relative to single-proxy models, or if the sign of the average return is not positive in recovered-growth regimes.

mechanism_narrative: An accelerating unemployment rate signals rising recession risk and margin pressure, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next 5 trading days when UNRATE breaks above its recent trend.
failure_condition: Reject if the 5d forward S&P 500 return is positive in more than half of observations where UNRATE has risen by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months, or if the conditional mean return is not statistically below zero.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP growth is positive and retail sales are also expanding, the economy is delivering demand momentum that supports earnings revisions and higher S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with concurrent positive GDPC1 and RSAFS growth fail to produce above-median 60d S&P 500 returns, or if the interaction term adds no incremental predictive value versus either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: In a disinflation regime, slower CPI growth combined with unchanged or lower FEDFUNDS eases real borrowing costs and supports equity multiples, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined low-inflation/steady-rate condition does not outperform a rate-only or inflation-only model in walk-forward testing, or if triggered 20d returns are not positive in at least 55% of instances.
